title: Preserve current Ollama mount mode in agent ollama-preload (avoid unnecessary recreate)
description:
Context: ./agent ollama-preload currently forces OLLAMA_MODELS_MOUNT_MODE=rw then (by default) locks back to ro, which can trigger unnecessary container recreates and unexpected mode changes when runtime is already rw. In rootless setups this can surface permission-denied behavior during model pull flows and leave operator confusion.

Scope:
- Detect current effective ollama model mount mode before preload.
- If already rw, skip mount-mode switching/recreate steps.
- If initially ro, switch to rw only for preload and restore original mode after preload (preserve prior state).
- Keep runtime.env coherent with effective mode.
- Update operator docs to describe state-preserving behavior.

Acceptance:
- Preload in rw mode does not recreate ollama and does not end by forcing ro.
- Preload in ro mode still performs temporary rw switch and restores ro.
- Existing preload success output reports final mode accurately.
close_reason: Implemented and documented in preload script, runbook, and PLAN.

comments.author: vuissoz
comments.text: Implemented state-preserving ollama preload behavior. Changes: (1) detect initial effective mount mode from running container when available, fallback to configured runtime.env mode; (2) skip mount-mode recreate when already rw; (3) if initially ro, switch to rw only for preload and restore initial mode by default; (4) keep --no-lock-ro override to keep rw after temporary switch; (5) add early writable-path check with actionable permission error; (6) update docs/runbooks/ollama-model-store.md and README.md; (7) reference issue in PLAN.md backlog.

title: Codex/OpenHands local qwen3.5:35b emits pseudo tool tags instead of real tool calls
description:
## Summary
With local model `qwen3.5:35b`, Codex/OpenHands sessions may emit pseudo tool tags such as `<read_file>...</read_file>` instead of executing real tool calls.

## Impact
- Agent stops after plain-text pseudo tool markup.
- No `exec_command` / `apply_patch` execution events are emitted.
- User perceives the stack as "missing tools" while tooling is present.

## Reproduction
1. Start stack in `rootless-dev` profile.
2. Use Codex with model `qwen3.5:35b` and prompt: `Write a Readme.md file`.
3. Observe output similar to:
   - `I'll help you create a README.md...`
   - `<read_file><path>/workspace/dgx-spark-agentic-stack</path></read_file>`
4. Session completes without actual tool invocation.

## Observed evidence
- Session rollout records assistant plain text pseudo tool markup and immediate task completion.
- `codex exec --full-auto` still returns plain text command content (`pwd`) instead of running tools.
- L6 model-catalog test passes, so provider/catalog wiring is not the failing layer.

## Scope / Likely cause
Model compatibility issue for tool-calling behavior with `qwen3.5:35b` in Codex/OpenHands flows (not a missing tool installation in stack).

## Suggested next steps
- Validate with a coding/tool-use-oriented model (e.g. `qwen3-coder*` / `qwen2.5-coder*`) and compare tool-call execution rate.
- Add a regression test that asserts at least one real tool execution event for a minimal coding task.
- Document the limitation in runbooks for local-model compatibility.
close_reason: Fixed in ollama-gate and verified live in rootless-dev for qwen3.5:35b across responses, chat/completions, and codex exec.

comments.author: vuissoz
comments.text: Implemented default-model guardrails for tool-calling compatibility. Onboarding now rejects qwen3.5:35b for AGENTIC_DEFAULT_MODEL and OpenHands LLM_MODEL, doctor fails explicitly on incompatible runtime/default selections, and regression coverage was added in tests/00_model_tool_compat.sh plus tests/00_onboarding_env_wizard.sh.

comments.author: vuissoz
comments.text: Revised handling after checking Ollama upstream: Qwen 3.5 is advertised with tools support on ollama.com, so the repo no longer hard-blocks qwen3.5:35b. Onboarding/doctor now emit explicit stack regression warnings instead, and the issue is being reopened as an integration bug rather than treated as a permanent model incompatibility.

comments.author: vuissoz
comments.text:
Implemented and verified a stack-side fix in `ollama-gate`.

Changes shipped:
- added XML-style pseudo-tool parsing for payloads such as `<read_file><path>...</path></read_file>`,
- filtered pseudo-tool recovery against the declared tool names,
- shipped the parser in the `ollama-gate` image.

Live verification in `rootless-dev` on March 26, 2026:
- `/v1/responses` on `qwen3.5:35b` returned `function_call` `read_file`,
- `/v1/chat/completions` on `qwen3.5:35b` returned `tool_calls`,
- `codex exec -m qwen3.5:35b` emitted a real `command_execution` (`cat /workspace/.qwen35-live-probe/token.txt`) and returned the exact token.

title: rootless-dev git-forge bootstrap stalls on Forgejo must-change-password admin policy
description:
## Summary
`AGENTIC_OPTIONAL_MODULES=mcp,git-forge,pi-mono,goose,portainer ./agent rootless-dev up optional` starts all optional containers, but the stack-managed git-forge bootstrap does not finish in `rootless-dev`.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start from a rootless-dev runtime with optional requests present.
2. Run `./agent rootless-dev update`.
3. Run `AGENTIC_SKIP_OPTIONAL_GATING=1 AGENTIC_OPTIONAL_MODULES=mcp,git-forge,pi-mono,goose,portainer ./agent rootless-dev up optional`.
4. Observe Forgejo accounts/team creation begins, then bootstrap fails.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `agent rootless-dev up optional` completes successfully with `git-forge` included.
- Forgejo admin bootstrap does not require a manual password-change workaround.
- `${AGENTIC_ROOT}/optional/git/bootstrap/git-forge-bootstrap.json` is written after bootstrap.
- The bootstrap handles non-writable service homes in rootless-dev without manual repair steps.

## Notes
Observed on 2026-03-29 in `/home/vuissoz/.local/share/agentic`:
- initial drift: `secrets/runtime/git-forge/*.password` existed as root-owned directories, blocking optional runtime init.
- after conservative runtime repair, `optional-forgejo`, `optional-pi-mono`, `optional-goose`, `optional-mcp-catalog`, `optional-portainer`, and `optional-sentinel` all reached healthy.
- stack-managed bootstrap still fails because Forgejo API returns `403 You must change your password` for `system-manager`, and `${AGENTIC_ROOT}/optional/git/bootstrap/git-forge-bootstrap.json` remains absent.

title: Implementation Plan
OpenHands V1 conversations stay disconnected due missing webhooks and legacy message payload mismatch
description:
Context:
- In rootless-dev, clicking "New conversation" opens `/conversations/<id>` but conversation stays disconnected/stuck.
- `/api/conversations/{id}/message` returned success while `/api/v1/conversation/{id}/events/search` stayed empty.

Root causes:
1. OpenHands service env lacked `OH_WEBHOOKS_*`, so per-sandbox agent-server never posted events back to `/api/v1/webhooks/events/{conversation_id}`.
2. Legacy bridge forwarded V0-style payloads (`message`/`action`/`args`) directly to V1 `SendMessageRequest`, producing empty `content=[]` user messages and no useful progress.

Fix implemented in repo:
- Set webhook envs in `compose/compose.ui.yml` (`OH_WEBHOOKS_0_BASE_URL`, buffer size, flush delay).
- Normalize bridge payloads in `deployments/patches/openhands/conversation.py` to V1 schema with `run=true`.
- Add regression assertion in `tests/H2_openhands.sh` to verify app event stream contains posted message text.
close_reason: implemented and verified via agent rootless-dev test H

title: Vérifier la logique start/stop des agents et leurs interdépendances
description:
Contexte:
Les nouvelles commandes `agent start/stop` doivent permettre d’arrêter/redémarrer un agent (service ou conteneur) sans casser le fonctionnement global de la stack.

Problème à vérifier:
- interactions entre agents, core (ollama-gate/proxy), UI et modules optionnels après un `stop/start` ciblé;
- effet de bord potentiel sur la santé des autres services (healthchecks, réseau, sessions tmux, doctor);
- robustesse des commandes en cas d’enchaînement rapide stop/start et arrêt partiel.

Objectif:
Confirmer que les commandes sont sûres et n’introduisent pas de régression opérationnelle sur l’ensemble de la stack.

Pistes d’acceptation:
1. stop/start d’un agent unique n’impacte pas les autres services.
2. `agent doctor` reste vert (ou warnings attendus selon profil) après cycles ciblés.
3. les sessions/outils redémarrent proprement sans intervention manuelle.
4. documenter les dépendances explicites et comportements attendus en cas d’arrêt partiel.
close_reason: Verified targeted agent stop/start isolation and landed regression coverage (L11 + ADR-0088).

comments.author: vuissoz
comments.text: Verification done in rootless-dev. Findings: agentic-{claude,codex,opencode,vibestral} have no Compose-level interdependency; coupling is runtime-only through core services on the shared network (ollama-gate, gate-mcp, egress-proxy, unbound). Manual check on the live rootless-dev stack showed agent ls healthy and agent doctor READY before changes. Landed: configurable OLLAMA_HOST_PORT for isolated core tests, ADR-0088 documenting the contract, and tests/L11_agent_start_stop_isolation.sh covering stop/start via tool, service, and container commands while asserting peer/core health and tmux session recovery.

comments.author: vuissoz
comments.text: Extended the stop/start surface beyond CLI agents. Landed first-class 'agent start/stop <target>' support for openwebui, openhands, and comfyui, plus bundled restart semantics for openclaw and comfyui. Added tests/L12_surface_stop_isolation.sh, which boots core+agents+ui+optional(goose) in disposable rootless-dev and verifies each target can be stopped and restarted independently while the rest of the stack stays healthy.

title: OpenHands callback bridge still emits 429 + event_callback_result integrity errors
description:
Context:
- Added legacy `/api/conversations/{id}/generate_title` V1 forwarder to stop previous 405 callback failures.
- Callback failures persist intermittently under load due local rate limiting and SQL callback result insert behavior.

Observed:
- `POST /api/conversations/<id>/generate_title` sometimes returns `429 Too Many Requests`.
- `sql_event_callback_service.py:231` logs repeated exceptions.
- Follow-on DB failure: `sqlite3.IntegrityError: NOT NULL constraint failed: event_callback_result.id`.

Impact:
- Noisy error logs during normal runs and callback processing instability.
- Can mask real runtime failures and degrade long-running sessions.

Expected:
- Internal callback path should not be rate-limited to 429 under normal event throughput.
- Callback result persistence should not fail on missing PK default.

title: OpenHands websocket requests still hit staticfiles route (AssertionError spam)
description:
Context:
- After fixing V1 message bridge + webhook sync, conversations now progress.
- OpenHands logs still emit recurring websocket tracebacks:
  `starlette/staticfiles.py:91 assert scope["type"] == "http"`
- This indicates websocket traffic is routed to static file handler (likely unmatched ws endpoint path in UI).

Impact:
- Functional chat path works, but logs are noisy and may mask real runtime failures.
- Potential latent UX instability if frontend depends on that websocket channel.

Expected:

title: OpenHands rootless-dev: new conversation startup can trigger OOM/restart
description:
Context
- In rootless-dev, creating new OpenHands conversations can fail intermittently.
- Reproduction observed on 2026-03-07: repeated POST /api/v1/app-conversations then polling /api/v1/app-conversations/start-tasks leads to container restart.

Evidence
- docker events shows: container oom + die exitCode=137 for agentic-dev-openhands-1.
- User-visible symptom: new conversation does not start properly / connection closed.

Expected
- Starting new conversations must not restart OpenHands container under normal single-user usage.

Scope
- Add runtime guardrails to avoid unbounded process sandbox accumulation and improve default memory headroom.
- Add regression test coverage for app-conversation start flow and restart detection.
close_reason: Fixed: bounded OpenHands process sandboxes + memory headroom + H2 regression test for conversation startup stability.

comments.author: vuissoz
comments.text:
Implemented fix and regression coverage.

- process_sandbox_service patch: enforce bounded per-user active process sandboxes (oldest sandbox cleanup before starting a new one).

title: OpenClaw CLI wizard parity in container (onboard/configure/agents add)
description:
Contexte:
Le runbook OpenClaw upstream (wizard) decrit un flux CLI operateur dans lequel on execute `openclaw onboard`, `openclaw configure` et `openclaw agents add <name>`.
Dans la stack DGX actuelle, OpenClaw est surtout opere via API/runtime et il manque une experience conteneurisee equivalente aux autres agents (session shell + workspace persistant + configuration standard).

Objectif:
Ajouter un conteneur OpenClaw operable comme les autres agents de la stack, permettant de se connecter au conteneur et d'executer le wizard CLI OpenClaw (`openclaw onboard`, `openclaw configure`, `openclaw agents add <name>`) dans un workspace persistant.

References:
- https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/wizard

Exigences:
- OpenClaw doit etre expose via une commande operateur coherente avec les autres agents (session dans conteneur + workspace de projet persistant).
- Les chemins d'etat/logs/workspaces doivent respecter les conventions `/srv/agentic/...` de la stack.
- Le conteneur OpenClaw doit conserver les garde-fous standards (loopback-only, pas de docker.sock, hardening compose usuel, secrets runtime hors git).
- La configuration standard (env/runtime/profiles) doit etre disponible pour OpenClaw comme pour les autres agents.

Resultat attendu:
Un operateur peut lancer une session dans le conteneur OpenClaw, executer le wizard OpenClaw de bout en bout, et retrouver ses artefacts/configuration dans les emplacements persistants standard de la stack.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Une commande operateur permet d'entrer dans le conteneur OpenClaw (tmux/shell) avec workspace persistant, au meme niveau d'ergonomie que les autres agents.
2) Depuis cette session, `openclaw onboard`, `openclaw configure` et `openclaw agents add <name>` s'executent avec succes sur un projet de test.
3) Les artefacts produits (config/state/workspace) persistent sous `/srv/agentic/openclaw/{state,logs,workspaces}` (ou conventions runtime equivalentes rootless documentees).
4) `agent doctor` couvre la presence/contrat de cette capacite OpenClaw CLI (mounts, utilisateur, hardening, loopback, absence docker.sock).
5) `agent update`/`agent rollback all <release_id>` preservent/restaurent la capacite sans drift de configuration.
6) Un runbook explique le flux wizard en conteneur et la procedure de verification.
close_reason: Implemented and validated

title: first-up: rootless-dev should not fail doctor on initial unresolved latest release
description:
Probleme:
En profil `rootless-dev`, un premier lancement via `./agent first-up` peut echouer a l'etape `doctor` alors que le runtime vient d'etre initialise et qu'aucun `agent update` n'a encore ete execute.

Reproduction constatee:
1. utiliser `AGENTIC_PROFILE=rootless-dev`
2. lancer `./agent first-up`
3. observer l'etape `doctor`

Symptomes observes:
- `doctor` detecte correctement les checks rootless degrades (DOCKER-USER / proxy enforcement)
- mais il traite l'absence de `deployments/current/latest-resolution.json` comme un echec bloquant si la release active contient encore des valeurs `latest`
- message final:
  - `FAIL: active release contains mutable 'latest' values that were not resolved deterministically; run 'agent update'`
  - `WARN: doctor result: NOT READY`
  - `ERROR: first-up failed at step 'doctor'`

Contexte:
- le comportement est incoherent avec le contrat de premier demarrage en `rootless-dev`: `first-up` ne doit pas exiger un `agent update` prealable pour etre utilisable
- le suivi `dgx-spark-agentic-stack-fcb` a introduit la resolution deterministe des `latest` via `agent update`, et `dgx-spark-agentic-stack-886` couvre deja les chemins hors `agent update`; ce bug est une manifestation concrete de ce trou de contrat sur le chemin `first-up`/premiere release rootless

Pistes de correction:
- soit faire produire un artefact `latest-resolution.json` coherent lors du chemin de bootstrap/first-up initial
- soit degrader ce cas en warning non bloquant pour une premiere release `rootless-dev` non encore passee par `agent update`
- dans tous les cas, conserver l'echec bloquant pour les derives reelles en mode normal/strict-prod ou pour une release qui pretend etre deterministe sans preuve

Impact:
- regression UX/operator sur le premier demarrage local
- `first-up` n'est plus fiable comme chemin d'onboarding rootless
acceptance_criteria:
- `AGENTIC_PROFILE=rootless-dev ./agent first-up` does not fail solely because the initial active release lacks `latest-resolution.json`
- `agent doctor` keeps a non-blocking signal for this first-run rootless case, with an actionable message
- deterministic `latest` enforcement remains blocking after `agent update` and in the intended strict paths
- regression coverage is added for the rootless first-up path (or existing `F9`/doctor coverage is extended accordingly)

title: Implement full J4 hybrid retrieval beyond skeleton
description:
Context
- J3/J4 skeleton has been added (schema + retriever/worker contract + optional opensearch profile), but retrieval is still non-functional by design (`status=skeleton`).

Problem
- Current `rag-retriever` does not execute real dense/lexical queries, does not fuse ranked lists from real backends, and does not rerank final candidates.

Scope
1. Implement dense retrieval against qdrant.
2. Implement lexical retrieval path (`opensearch` BM25 profile and/or qdrant sparse), with explicit backend selection.
3. Implement score/rank fusion (RRF baseline) with deterministic tie-break behavior.
4. Add optional reranker stage for top-N candidate refinement.
5. Extend tests from skeleton contract to functional relevance checks on technical tokens (acronyms, identifiers, versions).

Notes
- Depends on J3 canonical schema alignment and should update ingest/query scripts accordingly.
- Coordinate with umbrella `dgx-spark-agentic-stack-de9`.
acceptance_criteria: tests/J4_rag_hybrid_skeleton.sh evolves to functional hybrid retrieval assertions; dense+lexical fusion returns expected hits for lexical-sensitive queries; optional reranker path is tested and documented.

comments.author: vuissoz
comments.text: Scope decomposed on 2026-03-29 into child tasks: 9gz.1 operator ingestion UX, 9gz.2 hybrid relevance tests, 9gz.3 optional reranker, 9gz.4 OpenSearch lexical hardening. Parent remains open as umbrella until remaining J4 gaps are closed.

title: Add operator-facing RAG ingestion command and task UX
description:
Context
- RAG ingestion already exists in two forms:
  - standalone script (`deployments/rag/ingest.sh`) for direct Qdrant population,
  - internal worker API (`rag-worker` `POST /v1/index`) for async/sync indexing.
- Today this is functional but not ergonomic for operators. The normal entrypoint of the stack is `./agent`, not direct script execution or manual HTTP calls inside containers.

Problem
- There is no clear operator-facing command to trigger indexing, select a docs directory, wait for completion, inspect task status, or surface actionable failures.
- This makes the ingestion path look "missing" even though the plumbing exists.

Scope
1. Add an operator command such as `./agent rag index [--docs-dir ...] [--sync] [--wait]`.
2. Expose task lifecycle and status in a human-readable way:
   - accepted,
   - running,
   - completed,
   - error.
3. Ensure collection/index bootstrap remains automatic when missing.
4. Document the ingestion flow for:
   - dense-only baseline,
   - lexical mode with `rag-lexical`,
   - expected corpus location under `${AGENTIC_ROOT}/rag/docs`.

Notes
- Reuse existing `rag-worker` `/v1/index` task API instead of introducing a second orchestration path.
- Keep the command internal-only and compatible with the stack security baseline.
- Coordinate with umbrella `dgx-spark-agentic-stack-9gz`.
acceptance_criteria:
- `./agent rag index` exists and successfully triggers indexing through `rag-worker`.
- Operators can specify an alternate docs directory and optionally wait for task completion.
- Success and failure output is actionable and includes task id/status when relevant.
- Qdrant collection creation remains automatic if absent.
- When `RAG_LEXICAL_BACKEND=opensearch` is enabled, lexical indexing is also triggered through the same operator flow.
- README/runbook documentation is updated with the supported operator workflow.

title: Strengthen J4 hybrid retrieval tests with lexical-sensitive relevance assertions
description:
Context
- Current J4 tests validate startup, security baseline, indexing, endpoint contract, fusion presence, and audit logging.
- This proves the pipeline runs, but it does not yet prove that hybrid retrieval is meaningfully relevant on lexical-sensitive queries.

Problem
- The current J4 smoke checks are too weak for the original goal of "full hybrid retrieval beyond skeleton".
- We still lack deterministic relevance-oriented assertions for:
  - acronyms,
  - identifiers,
  - filenames,
  - versions,
  - CLI flags / technical tokens.
- As a result, the lexical side can exist without being strongly validated.

Scope
1. Add a deterministic mini-corpus designed to separate lexical and dense behavior.
2. Add retrieval assertions covering:
   - dense-only baseline,
   - lexical-only signal contribution when `rag-lexical` is enabled,
   - fused ranking behavior (`rrf`).
3. Validate that lexical-sensitive queries return expected top hits or expected hit sets.
4. Keep tests stable in dry-run environments and document any determinism constraints.

Notes
- The goal is not generic IR benchmarking; it is regression coverage for this repo's implemented retrieval path.
- Coordinate with umbrella `dgx-spark-agentic-stack-9gz`.
acceptance_criteria:
- J4 test coverage includes lexical-sensitive queries with expected relevant hits.
- Test corpus includes at least identifiers/acronyms/versions where lexical search materially matters.
- Results are checked beyond "non-empty": expected document/chunk ids, filenames, or source paths must be asserted.
- Coverage exists for both:
  - baseline dense mode without `rag-lexical`,
  - hybrid mode with `rag-lexical`.
- Fusion behavior remains deterministic enough for CI/rootless validation and is documented where assumptions apply.

title: Implement optional reranker stage for J4 hybrid retrieval
description:
Context
- `rag-retriever` currently performs dense retrieval, optional lexical retrieval, and `rrf` fusion.
- The response contract already includes a `rerank` block, but it is still a placeholder (`enabled=false`, empty results).

Problem
- The original J4 scope explicitly called for an optional reranker stage for top-N candidate refinement.
- Without it, the issue remains only partially complete and the response schema over-promises a feature that does not yet exist.

Scope
1. Implement a real optional reranker stage after fusion.
2. Add configuration for:
   - enable/disable,
   - candidate pool size,
   - top-N output,
   - backend/model selection.
3. Define safe fallback behavior when reranker backend/model is unavailable.
4. Extend tests to cover:
   - reranker disabled,
   - reranker enabled,
   - backend unavailable / degraded path.
5. Document latency/cost/tradeoff expectations.

Notes
- Keep reranking internal-only and consistent with the existing egress/security posture.
- The baseline path must remain functional when reranking is disabled.
- Coordinate with umbrella `dgx-spark-agentic-stack-9gz`.
acceptance_criteria:
- `/v1/retrieve` returns a real rerank stage when enabled.
- Reranker activation is configurable without breaking the default baseline path.
- If the reranker backend is unavailable, the API degrades gracefully and still returns fused results with an explicit status/signal.
- Automated tests cover enabled/disabled/degraded reranker behavior.
- Docs explain when to enable reranking and the expected latency tradeoff.

title: Harden OpenSearch lexical backend bootstrap and operational behavior
description:
Context
- Lexical retrieval is currently implemented through optional OpenSearch activation (`rag-lexical` profile).
- The worker writes documents and the retriever queries them, but bootstrap/mapping/operational handling are still fairly minimal.

Problem
- The lexical backend works, but production confidence is lower than it should be:
  - index bootstrap is implicit,
  - mapping behavior is not explicitly controlled enough,
  - indexing/query failures could be surfaced more clearly,
  - operator troubleshooting can be harder than necessary.

Scope
1. Make lexical index bootstrap explicit and reproducible.
2. Define and apply explicit OpenSearch mappings/settings suitable for current payload structure.
3. Improve indexing/query error surfacing in worker/retriever responses and logs.
4. Extend operational documentation for:
   - first enablement,
   - reindexing,
   - failure diagnosis,
   - persistence expectations.
5. Preserve internal-only exposure and existing hardening constraints.

Notes
- This issue is about hardening and determinism, not replacing OpenSearch with another lexical backend.
- Coordinate with umbrella `dgx-spark-agentic-stack-9gz`.
acceptance_criteria:
- OpenSearch lexical index bootstrap is explicit and repeatable.
- Index mappings/settings are defined intentionally rather than relying only on ad hoc dynamic behavior.
- Lexical indexing failures produce actionable worker errors/logs.
- Lexical query failures produce actionable retriever errors/logs.
- Documentation covers enablement, reindexing, persistence paths, and troubleshooting for `rag-lexical`.

title: Créer une image agent-cli-base professionnelle (NVIDIA CUDA + toolchain dev complète)
description:
Contexte: la base actuelle des agents est trop minimale (bash/git/curl/tmux) et ne permet pas un workflow de développement professionnel C/C++/Python/Node/CUDA sans bootstrap manuel fragile.

Objectif: faire évoluer l'image commune agent-cli-base pour qu'elle soit directement exploitable sur DGX Spark pour du développement professionnel, tout en conservant les invariants sécurité et traçabilité du CDC.

Périmètre attendu:
- Basculer l'image de base vers une image NVIDIA CUDA devel adaptée DGX Spark (ARM64), avec version explicitement épinglée.
- Préinstaller le socle dev: build-essential, cmake, ninja, pkg-config, python3+venv+pip, nodejs+npm, go, rust/cargo, git-lfs, ripgrep, fd, jq, shellcheck, shfmt, openssh-client, rsync, direnv, etc.
- Préinstaller le socle C/C++ pro: gdb/gdbserver, valgrind, clang/clangd/lld/lldb, clang-format, clang-tidy, cppcheck, ccache, bear, meson/autotools.
- Vérifier disponibilité toolchain CUDA (nvcc + libs de dev) dans les agents, sans ouvrir docker.sock ni privilèges supplémentaires.
- Conserver non-root, read_only, cap_drop=ALL, no-new-privileges, et compatibilité update/rollback digest.
- Documenter la matrice versions (base image, CUDA, outils clés) + stratégie de mise à jour.

Critères d'acceptation:
1) build de l'image réussi sur cible DGX Spark ARM64.
2) dans agentic-codex: commandes clés disponibles (gcc/g++, cmake, ninja, clang, python3, pip, node, npm, go, rustc, cargo, nvcc).
3) tests de sécurité doctor inchangés (pas de régression).
4) update enregistre les digests exacts déployés; rollback restaure l'image précédente.
5) documentation runbook/README mise à jour pour usage et limites.
notes: Implémentation en cours: Dockerfile agent-cli-base migré vers nvidia/cuda:13.0.1-devel-ubuntu24.04 (digest pin) avec toolchain dev pro C/C++/Python/Node/Go/Rust + CUDA; tests E1 et E1b passés; README FR/EN + ADR-0032 ajoutés. title: Periodic review: refactoring strategy vs current code
description:
## Context
A refactoring strategy runbook exists at `docs/runbooks/implementation-strategy-refactoring.md`.
The codebase evolves quickly, so this strategy can become stale.

## Goal
Periodically re-evaluate the refactoring strategy against the current implementation and adjust priorities based on real risk and drift.

## Review cadence
Quarterly (every 90 days), or earlier after major architecture changes.

## Review checklist
- Re-assess the top priorities against current code hotspots (`scripts/agent.sh`, runtime env schema handling, compose duplication, doctor/static policy consistency, rollback determinism).
- Validate whether rollback is fully hermetic from release artifacts only.
- Confirm the documented priorities still match operational risk and maintenance pain.
- Update `docs/runbooks/implementation-strategy-refactoring.md` if priorities or sequencing changed.
- If needed, open/close/supersede specific implementation issues in Beads.

## Acceptance criteria
- A dated review note is added or linked from the runbook.
- Any strategy changes are committed in docs.
- Follow-up implementation issues are created/updated in Beads.
- Next review due date is set.
acceptance_criteria: Quarterly review completed, runbook updated if needed, and next review date set

title: Produire des runbooks debutants par service (usage pas a pas)
description:
Contexte
- Les runbooks actuels expliquent bien l'architecture globale, mais un debutant a besoin d'un guide d'usage concret service par service.
- Objectif: fournir, pour chaque service expose a l'operateur, un runbook oriente usage avec scenarios simples, commandes minimales et depannage de premier niveau.

Scope
1. Definir un template unique "runbook debutant par service" (objectif, prerequis, comment y acceder local/Tailscale+SSH, actions de base, erreurs frequentes, verification rapide).
2. Creer/mettre a jour un runbook pour chaque service baseline: Ollama, OpenWebUI, OpenHands, ComfyUI, agentic-claude, agentic-codex, observabilite (Prometheus/Grafana/Loki), gate/proxy.
3. Ajouter des exemples ultra concrets (commandes exactes, URLs localhost via tunnel, workflow minimal en 5-10 minutes).
4. Ajouter une section "Depuis un poste externe via Tailscale" pour chaque service concerne (CLI et UI).
5. Relier ces runbooks depuis README et docs/runbooks/introduction.md.

Contraintes
- Garder le modele securite du projet: loopback-only, pas de docker.sock, acces distant via Tailscale+SSH.
- Vocabulaire debutant: eviter le jargon non explique.
acceptance_criteria:
- Un runbook debutant existe pour chaque service baseline liste dans le scope.
- Chaque runbook inclut un parcours "premiere utilisation" testable en autonomie.
- Chaque runbook inclut acces externe via Tailscale+SSH (quand applicable) avec commandes exactes.
- README et introduction pointent vers ces runbooks de facon claire.
- Relecture de coherence FR/EN effectuee (ou backlog explicite si EN en second temps).

title: OpenClaw onboarding wizard mismatch with stack-managed workspace and gateway health
description:
## Summary
The upstream `openclaw onboard` wizard is still misleading in this repository. It offers `/state/cli/openclaw-home/.openclaw/workspace` as the default workspace and performs a gateway health probe against `127.0.0.1:8111`, while the stack requires persisted operator workspaces under `/workspace/...` and exposes the managed upstream gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789`.

## Impact
New operators can complete onboarding, then see:
- `/overlay/openclaw.operator-overlay.json: agents.defaults.workspace must stay under /workspace/`
- `Health check failed: gateway closed (1006...)` against `ws://127.0.0.1:8111`
- misleading follow-up errors if they manually run `openclaw gateway run` and hit Anthropic defaults

## Desired outcome
Make the stack path harder to misuse by either enforcing `/workspace/...` in the wizard/on wrapper path, or by adding a managed onboarding helper that skips the upstream health probe and avoids the unmanaged gateway path entirely.

## Acceptance criteria
- A first-time operator cannot accidentally persist `agents.defaults.workspace` outside `/workspace/...`.
- Stack docs no longer need to rely on manual interpretation of the upstream `8111` health probe.
- A supported onboarding path exists that does not suggest `openclaw gateway run` for normal stack operation.
notes: Fixed via commit 6ef58cd: managed OpenClaw init now repairs the default workspace back under /workspace/... and demotes the upstream wizard/gateway path to expert fallback.

title: OpenHands rootless-dev stuck on landing screen (conversation message/event 404)
description:
Context
- Profile: rootless-dev
- Symptom: OpenHands UI remains on "Let's start building!" and does not progress when starting/sending a first message.

Observed evidence
- Start-task creation reaches READY via /api/v1/app-conversations/start-tasks.
- Subsequent message/event posts fail with 404:
  - POST /api/conversations/<id>/message -> 404
  - POST /api/conversations/<id>/events -> 404
- Container logs show: "get_conversation: conversation <id> not found, attach_to_conversation returned None".

Impact
- Fresh conversation creation appears to work, but chat interaction fails immediately, leaving UI unusable for normal startup flow.

Requested fix
- Add V1-aware handling for legacy /api/conversations/{id}/message and /events routes by resolving app-conversation metadata and forwarding to conversation_url with session API key.
- Keep V0 behavior unchanged.
acceptance_criteria:
- Reproducing POST /api/conversations/<id>/message in rootless-dev returns 200 for a READY V1 conversation.
- Reproducing POST /api/conversations/<id>/events in rootless-dev returns 200 for a READY V1 conversation.
- OpenHands UI can leave the landing screen and send the first message.
close_reason: Fixed by adding V1 bridge for /api/conversations/{id}/message and /events plus regression test in H2_openhands.

title: ollama-gate /v1/chat/completions ignores stream=true and breaks vibe interactive
description:
## Summary
In rootless-dev, Vibestral interactive mode (`vibe`) fails through ollama-gate with:

Error: API error from ollama-gate (model: qwen3-coder:30b): Stream chunk improperly formatted. Expected 'key: value' ...

## Reproduction
1. export AGENTIC_PROFILE=rootless-dev
2. ./agent vibestral workspace-default-python
3. In interactive mode run vibe
4. Observe streaming parse failure

## Technical cause
`/v1/chat/completions` in `deployments/gate/app.py` hardcodes stream=False and returns JSON even when payload has "stream": true.
Clients expecting SSE parse raw JSON as malformed stream chunk.

## Acceptance criteria
- /v1/chat/completions honors stream=true
- Response is SSE (text/event-stream) with data: ... frames and data: [DONE]
- Vibestral interactive mode no longer fails with malformed stream chunk error
- Add regression coverage in gate protocol compatibility tests
close_reason: Fixed in code: chat completions streaming SSE + D8 regression

title: Clarify mvance/unbound digest pinning for rollback
description:
Le 9 fevrier 2026, verification locale de `docker manifest inspect --verbose mvance/unbound:latest`:

- Le tag `mvance/unbound:latest` retourne un manifest Docker V2 simple (`application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json`), pas un index multi-arch.
- Le digest a pinner pour un deploiement reproductible est le digest du descriptor/manifest: `sha256:76906da36d1806f3387338f15dcf8b357c51ce6897fb6450d6ce010460927e90`.
- Le digest `sha256:a7778ebb14b25328fc6de5b58ddf5dfe3e0891afec8fd86744995fd8dbcd5b4e` est `config.digest`; il ne doit pas etre utilise pour le pinning d'image Compose.
- Exemple correct:
  `image: mvance/unbound@sha256:76906da36d1806f3387338f15dcf8b357c51ce6897fb6450d6ce010460927e90`
- Contrainte plateforme observee: `linux/amd64`.

Impact CDC:
- `agent update`/snapshot digests doit stocker le digest de manifest deploye, pas le config digest.
- `agent rollback` doit repinner exactement ce manifest digest.
close_reason: Step B switched to multi-arch unbound image (klutchell/unbound); mvance-specific digest pinning is no longer applicable.

title: OpenClaw: implémenter un dashboard opérateur accessible via tunnel SSH/Tailscale
description:
Contexte:
La documentation upstream OpenClaw propose un mode dashboard (`openclaw gateway run --dashboard`).
Dans la stack actuelle, nous exposons surtout une API loopback OpenClaw (`127.0.0.1:${OPENCLAW_WEBHOOK_HOST_PORT:-18111}`) sans dashboard operateur equivalent.

Implementer un dashboard OpenClaw dans la stack DGX avec un acces operateur coherent avec les autres interfaces: via tunnel SSH/Tailscale vers des binds loopback uniquement.

Exigences de securite:
- aucune exposition publique directe (pas de bind hote sur 0.0.0.0),
- acces via tunnel SSH/Tailscale uniquement,

5) `agent update` et `agent rollback all <release_id>` conservent/restaurent le dashboard dans les artefacts release.

title: Clarify/suppress non-blocking setfacl warnings in rootless-dev runtime init
description:

In `rootless-dev`, some `agent` commands print:

- `setfacl: .../proxy/logs/cache.log: Operation not permitted`
Even when ACLs on the directory are already sufficient and execution continues.

## Problem
The warning looks like a hard failure and creates operator confusion during onboarding and preload runs.

## Expected
- Detect when file-level ACL update is impossible/non-required in non-root mode and avoid noisy stderr output.

- Emit a single explicit INFO/WARN line that this is non-blocking and why.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rootless run no longer prints raw `setfacl ... Operation not permitted` lines for this known non-blocking case.
- Runtime init still ensures required write access for Squid logs.
- A regression test or shell test covers the behavior.

title: Fix GitHub access blocked by SSH password prompt
description:
Context
- Git operations to GitHub are blocked/interrupted because remote access currently requires interactive SSH password authentication.
- This affects non-interactive workflows (agent automation, CI-like runs, and scripted `git pull --rebase` / `git push`).

Problem
- GitHub connection is behind SSH with password prompt.
- Automation cannot complete end-to-end publish/sync steps when credentials require manual prompt handling.

Impact
- `git push` and sync steps are unreliable in automated sessions.
- Work may remain stranded locally if push cannot complete unattended.

Proposed safe remediation
1. Prefer SSH key-based auth with `ssh-agent` (no password prompt during session).
2. If organization policy requires passphrase, preload agent once per session.
3. As fallback, use HTTPS remote with PAT via credential helper (non-interactive).

Acceptance criteria
- `git ls-remote origin` succeeds non-interactively.
- `git pull --rebase` and `git push` succeed without password prompt in the target workflow context.
- Runbook updated with the chosen auth method and recovery steps.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub pull/push operations work non-interactively in normal repo workflow
close_reason: Resolved: non-interactive SSH auth verified (git ls-remote with BatchMode + ssh -T) and runbook updated in docs/runbooks/first-time-setup.md section 2.5

title: Fix optional-goose rootless runtime paths under read-only rootfs
description:
Context
- In rootless-dev optional mode, `optional-goose` currently mounts only `/home/goose/.config/goose` writable while the container rootfs is read-only.
- Upstream Goose (v1.27.0) writes session DB and logs under `~/.local/share/goose` and `~/.local/state/goose` by default.

Observed failure
- Repro command:
  docker run --rm --read-only -v <tmp>:/home/goose/.config/goose ghcr.io/block/goose:latest session list
- Result: panic creating session DB directory on read-only filesystem.

Required fix
- Align `optional-goose` with rootless-safe runtime conventions used by other agents:
  - explicit non-root user mapping (`AGENT_RUNTIME_UID:AGENT_RUNTIME_GID`),
  - writable home/XDG paths under `/state/home`,
  - writable mounted state volume at `/state` (instead of only `~/.config`),
  - keep proxy/security baseline unchanged.
- Add doctor assertions for Goose HOME/XDG contract + writable state paths.
- Add integration test coverage for optional Goose activation and runtime writability.

Acceptance criteria
- `AGENTIC_OPTIONAL_MODULES=goose ./agent up optional` starts `optional-goose` healthy in rootless-dev.
- `goose session list` succeeds inside the container with read-only rootfs.
- doctor fails if Goose HOME/XDG/writable-state contract drifts.
- Test suite includes a Goose optional-module test.
close_reason: Implemented Goose rootless/runtime fix in compose, doctor checks, and K5 integration test.

title: Investigate missing host loopback listener for Forgejo port 13010
description:
`optional-forgejo` advertises a host binding `127.0.0.1:13010->3000`, but host-side connections to `http://127.0.0.1:13010/` still fail with connection refused while the service remains healthy on the internal Docker network. title: Add stack-managed OpenClaw onboarding/repair flow and demote upstream wizard to fallback
description:
## Summary
Beginner onboarding for OpenClaw should stop depending on operators driving the upstream wizard correctly. The stack should expose a managed command path for the safe baseline and keep the upstream wizard only as an expert fallback.

## Scope
Add a stack-managed flow centered on `./agent openclaw init` that:
- runs the known-safe base initialization path for this repository,
- is idempotent and safe to rerun as a repair operation,
- moves channel onboarding to file-backed stack config where possible,
- keeps direct upstream `openclaw onboard` / `openclaw gateway run` out of the beginner path.

## Requested behavior
1. Add `./agent openclaw init`
   - export the managed gateway token from the file-backed secret,
   - run the stack-safe OpenClaw bootstrap path with `/workspace/...` and the known-safe skips,
   - seed or reconcile a default operator workspace/agent,
   - print the exact next steps for provider/channel setup.
2. Make `init` rerunnable as repair
   - rerunning the command must repair common drift such as an invalid default workspace outside `/workspace/...`,
   - it must not require destructive cleanup for normal recovery,
   - it should be safe to run after an interrupted or partially broken onboarding.
3. Move channel onboarding to stack-managed file-backed config where possible
   - prefer `${AGENTIC_ROOT}/secrets/runtime/...` plus the provider bridge for Telegram/Discord/Slack,
   - keep QR/manual providers as explicit assisted follow-ups only when unavoidable.
4. Keep the upstream wizard as expert fallback only
   - docs should position `openclaw onboard` / `openclaw configure --section channels` as advanced/manual paths, not the default beginner path,
   - docs should explicitly say not to use `openclaw gateway run` for normal stack operation.

## Acceptance criteria
- `./agent openclaw init` exists and succeeds on a first-time stack with managed defaults.
- Rerunning `./agent openclaw init` repairs the common broken workspace case without destructive reset.
- Beginner docs use the stack command as the primary path.
- Telegram/Discord/Slack onboarding is documented and wired through file-backed stack config where supported.
- Upstream wizard usage remains available, but clearly labeled expert fallback.
notes: Implemented in commit 6ef58cd: added ./agent openclaw init, host-side overlay/state repair, K11 regression coverage, and docs/ADR updates.

title: UX: auto-resolve active profile for ./agent ls and similar read-only commands
description:
Context:
Today `./agent ls` uses `AGENTIC_PROFILE`, which defaults to `strict-prod` when unset. On machines primarily operated in `rootless-dev`, users often expect `./agent ls` to inspect the active local stack without having to prefix `rootless-dev` or export `AGENTIC_PROFILE` first.

Problem:
The current behavior is correct but ambiguous from a UX perspective: `./agent ls` can silently target the wrong runtime/project when `AGENTIC_PROFILE` is unset or the shell profile does not match the currently running stack.

Goal:
Make `./agent ls` and possibly other read-only inspection commands auto-resolve the active profile more intelligently, while keeping mutating commands explicit and safe.

Suggested direction:
- keep explicit prefix (`./agent rootless-dev ls`, `./agent strict-prod ls`) as highest priority;
- when no explicit profile is given, detect whether exactly one known stack runtime/project is active and use it;
- if both profiles appear active or detection is ambiguous, fail with a clear message asking for explicit profile selection;
- limit this UX shortcut to read-only commands first (`ls`, `status`, `ps`, maybe `logs`), not mutating commands.

Acceptance ideas:
1. `./agent ls` targets the obvious active profile when only one profile is running.
2. Ambiguous cases fail closed with an actionable message.
3. Explicit profile selection always overrides auto-detection.
4. Mutating commands remain explicit unless separately designed and approved.

title: Per-agent persistent /workspace host directories configured by onboarding
description:
Context
- Requirement is to configure persistent workspace host directories per agent container, not to set a default in-container project folder.
- Each agent container must mount its own dedicated host directory into /workspace, and onboarding must propose/store those paths.

Scope
1. Introduce explicit env vars per agent workspace mount:
   - AGENTIC_CLAUDE_WORKSPACES_DIR
   - AGENTIC_CODEX_WORKSPACES_DIR
   - AGENTIC_OPENCODE_WORKSPACES_DIR
   - AGENTIC_VIBESTRAL_WORKSPACES_DIR
2. Wire onboarding prompts/flags and generated env output for these variables.
3. Update compose mounts and runtime/bootstrap scripts to honor these per-container paths.
4. Remove AGENT_DEFAULT_PROJECT behavior introduced previously.
5. Update PLAN.md + tests/docs accordingly.
acceptance_criteria:
- onboarding asks/proposes per-agent workspace host directories with safe defaults.
- generated env exports all four AGENTIC_*_WORKSPACES_DIR variables.
- agentic-{claude,codex,opencode,vibestral} each mount /workspace from their own configured host path.
- no AGENT_DEFAULT_PROJECT variable remains in runtime/onboarding/docs.
- updated tests pass for onboarding + agent runtime behavior.
close_reason: Implemented and validated

title: Add optional Git forge service with per-agent accounts and shared project hosting
description:
Introduce a stack-managed self-hosted Git service so the operator and all agent surfaces can collaborate on shared repositories without relying on third-party hosting. The service must provide persistent repository and database storage, a loopback-only web UI for the host operator, and pre-provisioned accounts for openclaw, openhands, comfyui, claude, codex, opencode, vibestral, pi-mono, and goose.

Scope
- Add a new stack service/profile for a self-hosted Git forge (Forgejo/Gitea-class).
- Persist repositories and forge database under the AGENTIC_ROOT runtime contract.
- Expose the web UI on 127.0.0.1 only, reachable from the hosting system side.
- Bootstrap an operator account with system manager/admin role.
- Bootstrap per-agent service accounts with credentials materialized outside git so agent containers can clone/fetch/pull/push against the internal forge.
- Document operator lifecycle: first bootstrap, account rotation, repository creation, project sharing between agents, backup/restore expectations, and compliance checks.

Constraints
- No public bind, no docker.sock, no secrets in git.
- Remain compatible with strict-prod and rootless-dev runtime contracts.
- Keep traceability/rollback expectations aligned with the existing release system.
acceptance_criteria:
- a dedicated git forge service exists in the plan with explicit compose profile, persistence paths, healthchecks, and hardening expectations.
- the runtime contract includes persistent database and repository paths for the forge.
- the operator has a documented system manager/admin account bootstrap path.
- named accounts exist for openclaw, openhands, comfyui, claude, codex, opencode, vibestral, pi-mono, and goose, with documented credential handling outside git.
- documentation covers routine management and repo-sharing workflows between agents.
- PLAN.md references the feature and its Beads issue as pending work.
